step_1_context.text: When we give an account of a sequence of past events we usually put these events in chronological order using the past simple. If we want to refer to an event out of order – that is, an event which ha

step_2_explanation.simple_rule: When we give an account of a sequence of past events we usually put these events in chronological order using the past simple.
step_2_explanation.formula: If we want to refer to an event out of order – that is, an event which happened before the last event in the sequence we have written or spoken about – we can use the past perfect.
step_2_explanation.common_mistake:
When we understand that we are talking about events before another past event, we don't have to continue using the past perfect:
 • We bought a new car last month.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 5.1.1 In the story 'I first saw the old house I had just moved to the area', which event happened first?
options: I first saw the old house
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Past perfect ('had moved') indicates it happened before the past simple ('saw').

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 5.1.2 '...I learnt that a property developer had bought it...' Which event happened first?
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Past perfect shows the action completed before 'learnt'.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 5.1.3 'Six months later I had nearly given up hope... when I heard that the house was for sale again.' Which event happened first?
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Past perfect used for the earlier event.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 5.1.4 'The property developer had decided to invest his money in a new housing development... I bought the house immediately.' Which event is mentioned second but happened first?
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: The developer's decision precedes the purchase.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 5.2.1 As Jonas was introduced to Mrs Lopez, he realised that he ___ her before. (meet)
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Reporting a thought based on a present perfect original thought ('I have met her before').
